taskCaption: object ends up inside the container (terminal-relation-only diagnostic)

pilotMetadata.tests: Asserts, via the FROZEN matcher, ONLY that the mover's TERMINAL relation to the container is INSIDE (one hard OBJECT_RELATION_GOAL -> goal_satisfaction). It does NOT require an initial NEAR state, a NEAR->INSIDE transition, a CONTAINMENT_CHANGE event, contact, hand, or temporal order. The RH20T analogue of the real-camera/value-only target: it answers 'did the object end inside?' and nothing more.
pilotMetadata.enforcedVsAuthoring: ENFORCED: terminal INSIDE(mover,container) only (goal_satisfaction reads the robot's terminal relation in csg.matcher._goal_satisfaction_distance). AUTHORING ONLY (not verifier-enforced): geometry source/category, confidences. A 'born-inside' episode (object inside the whole time, but it MOVES so the extractor reports a terminal INSIDE) PASSes this target -- which is exactly why the strictly-stronger object_inside_container_relation_event target exists.
pilotMetadata.deferredNote: The human-reviewed RH20T annotation has no honest evidence for who/what moved the object (no tracked effector); this target deliberately asserts the weakest honest claim (terminal containment) so the relation-event target can be shown strictly stronger.
pilotMetadata.source: RH20T selected containment episode; raw media stays on RunPod. See datasets/rh20t_object_inside_container_v0/manifest.json.
pilotMetadata.notAGoldTask: Pilot diagnostic, deliberately NOT added to gold_tests/. Read it for terminal-containment mappability from an external recorded source, not as a full object_inside_container acceptance test. It asserts relation evidence only and does not assert contact causality.
